Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»REVEALED: What SA athletes will earn per medal at Olympics

SASCOC has revealed the bonus payment structure put in place for South African athletes who win medals at the 2024 Olympics in Paris.

South Africa will again have a host of athletes representing the countries in numerous events at the 2024 Olympics Games in Paris and SASCOC’S latest announcement has made it all the more enticing for them.

SASCOC —the South African Sports Confederation and Olympic Committee— on Wednesday announced its payment structure put in place for athletes that’ll secure medals at this year’s Olympic Games in the French capital, Paris.

As per SASCOC, all South Africans who win gold medals at this year’s Olympics will receive a whopping R400 000 payment.

Athletes who earn silver will get R200 000, while bronze medallists will receive R100 000.

Additionally, coaches will also be rewarded sufficiently for their contribution to these athletes’ success.

For every gold medal winner, its coach will bag R100 000, while silver and bronze will get these mentors R100 000 and R50 000, respectively.

SASCOC have announcedthe first batch of TeamSA athletes heading to the Paris Olympic Games.

The first official group bound for the Olympics are in seven disciplines comprising of aquatics, athletics, canoeing, gymnastics, sport climbing, surfing and wrestling.

Some of the big, most recognisable names on the list are Chad le Clos and Tatjana Smith (swimming); Wayde van Niekerk, Akine Simbine and Garda Steyn (athletics); and Jordy Smith (surfing).

title: Olympics: SASCOC reveals bonus payment structure for medallists
author: Mariette Adams
description: SASCOC has revealed the bonus payment structure put in place for South African athletes who win medals at the 2024 Olympics in Paris.
